# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 73

This is turn 73. Turns 1 through 72 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 73.

## Turn 73 — faithful-image projectivity makes the deep-unit proof exact

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. As before, the proof uses the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is only its required verifier residue. 

Turn 72 completed the candidate-side Wall–Arf calculation but left the local Kummer/deep-unit theorem among the pieces needing formal consolidation.  The vulnerable point is Turn 61’s passage from paired graded unit pieces to an actual deep \(H\)-equivariant class: without exactness of \(\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,-)\), a graded constituent need not lift through the unit filtration. 

That exactness is available after choosing the splitting field correctly.

### 1. Faithful tame images make ramified simple modules projective

Let \(V\) be a ramified simple \(\mathbf F _2\)-representation, and let

\[
H=\operatorname{im}(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\to\operatorname{GL}(V))
   =\langle S,T\rangle ,
\qquad S^{-1}TS=T^2,
\]

be its **faithful** finite tame image. Put \(e=\operatorname{ord}(T)\), and

\[
f=\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^a r,\qquad r\ \text{odd}.
\]

The finite tame group has the split form \(C_e\rtimes C_n\), with \(f\mid n\). Since \(S^f\) centralizes both \(S\) and \(T\), it lies in \(Z(H)\). By Schur’s lemma, its action belongs to

\[
\operatorname{End}_H(V)^\times,
\]

the multiplicative group of a finite field, and consequently has odd order. Faithfulness then implies

\[
\frac{n}{f}\ \text{is odd},
\qquad v_2(n)=v_2(f)=a.
\]

Let \(P\) be a Sylow \(2\)-subgroup of \(H\), so \(P\simeq C_{2^a}\).

Over \(\overline{\mathbf F}_2\), the inertia weights form the orbit

\[
\chi,\chi^2,\ldots,\chi^{2^{f-1}},
\]

each with a common multiplicity \(m\). The group \(P\) partitions these \(f\) weights into \(r\) cycles of length \(2^a\). Around each cycle the product of the transition maps is \(1\), because \(P^{2^a}=1\). After choosing bases successively around the cycle, its module is therefore

\[
\overline{\mathbf F}_2[P]^{\,m}.
\]

Hence

\[
V|_P\simeq \mathbf F_2[P]^{\,rm}
\]

after descent. Restriction to a Sylow \(2\)-subgroup detects projectivity, so:

\[
\boxed{V\ \text{and}\ V^\vee\ \text{are projective }\mathbf F_2[H]\text{-modules}.}
\tag{73.1}
\]

This also explains the apparent counterexample \(C_3\rtimes C_4\) from Turn 61: on the natural two-dimensional module the central \(C_2\) acts trivially, so that tame group is not the faithful image. Its faithful image is \(S_3\), and restriction to its Sylow \(C_2\) is the regular module.

### 2. The Kummer identification and unit-filtration count are now exact

Let \(K/\mathbf Q_2\) be the tame Galois extension fixed by the kernel of the action, so that \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbf Q_2)=H\). Projectivity gives

\[
H^i(H,V)=H^i(H,V^\vee)=0\qquad(i>0).
\]

Hochschild–Serre and Kummer theory therefore give the exact identification

\[
H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)
 \simeq
\operatorname{Hom}_H
 \bigl(V^\vee,K^\times/K^{\times2}\bigr).
\tag{73.2}
\]

More importantly, \(\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,-)\) is now an exact functor.

Write \(e=v_K(2)=|I|\), let \(M=K^\times/K^{\times2}\), and let \(\overline U_j\) be the image of \(1+\mathfrak p_K^j\) in \(M\). For odd \(1\le j<2e\),

\[
\operatorname{gr}_jM
   =\overline U_j/\overline U_{j+1}
   \simeq k_K,
\]

with tame inertia acting through the \(j\)-th power of the fundamental character. The even graded pieces vanish. The Hilbert pairing gives \(H\)-equivariant perfect dualities

\[
\operatorname{gr}_{j}M
   \times
\operatorname{gr}_{2e-j}M
   \longrightarrow \mathbf F_2.
\tag{73.3}
\]

The middle piece \(j=e\), the valuation piece, and the terminal unit piece all have trivial inertia, so none receives a map from the ramified module \(V^\vee\).

Put

\[
X=\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,M),\qquad
X_+=\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,\overline U_{e+1}).
\]

Exactness through the filtration gives

\[
\dim X
 =\sum_{\substack{1\le j<2e\\j\ \mathrm{odd},\,j\ne e}}
   \dim\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,\operatorname{gr}_jM),
\]

whereas \(X_+\) is the sum over \(e<j<2e\). By (73.3) and \(V\simeq V^\vee\), the \(j\)- and \(2e-j\)-summands have equal dimensions. Exactly one member of each pair lies above \(e\). Therefore

\[
\boxed{\dim X_+=\frac12\dim X.}
\tag{73.4}
\]

This removes the nonexact “choose a deep lift of the dual graded constituent” step.

### 3. The deep half is totally singular, not merely isotropic

Let \(q:V\to\mathbf F_2\) be an invariant nonsingular determinant form, and let

\[
Q_{\mathbf Q_2,q}:H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)\to
H^2(\mathbf Q_2,\mathbf F_2)\simeq\mathbf F_2
\]

be its connecting quadratic form.

Projectivity supplies an \(H\)-split embedding

\[
i:V\hookrightarrow \mathbf F_2[H]^N
\]

with \(H\)-linear projection \(p\). Extend \(q\) to the free module by

\[
\widetilde q=q\circ p.
\]

Under Shapiro, a class in \(i_*(X_+)\) is represented by scalar Kummer classes lying in \(\overline U_{e+1}\). An \(H\)-invariant quadratic polynomial on \(\mathbf F_2[H]^N\) is a sum of \(H\)-orbit sums of cross-monomials and square monomials. Cohomologically, these evaluate as corestrictions of scalar terms

\[
\alpha\smile g\beta
\quad\text{and}\quad
\alpha^2.
\]

All conjugates of \(\alpha,\beta\) remain in \(\overline U_{e+1}\). The dyadic Hilbert orthogonality relation is

\[
\overline U_i^{\,\perp}=\overline U_{2e-i+1}.
\]

Hence \(\overline U_{e+1}\subseteq\overline U_e
=\overline U_{e+1}^{\,\perp}\), so every cross-term vanishes. Moreover,

\[
\alpha^2=\alpha\smile(-1)=0,
\]

because \(-1\in\overline U_e\). Every orbit sum and its corestriction therefore vanish. By naturality,

\[
\boxed{Q_{\mathbf Q_2,q}|_{X_+}=0.}
\tag{73.5}
\]

The polarization of \(Q_{\mathbf Q_2,q}\) is the perfect local Tate pairing induced by the polar form of \(q\), so \(Q_{\mathbf Q_2,q}\) is nonsingular. Equations (73.4)–(73.5) exhibit a half-dimensional totally singular subspace. Consequently

\[
\boxed{\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{\mathbf Q_2,q})=0}
\]

for every ramified self-dual simple tame module.

Thus the local half of the determinant comparison is now exact:

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{\mathrm{local}})
=
\begin{cases}
1,&\text{unramified},\\
0,&\text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

Combined with Turn 72’s Wall-doubling theorem, the candidate has precisely the same two values. The repair is uniform: it simultaneously handles every ramified simple tame module and every scalar-twist branch, rather than adding another finite case.

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved that every ramified simple module is projective over its faithful finite tame image. This makes \(\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,-)\) exact, gives a rigorous half-dimensional count for the deep-unit Kummer subspace, and—via a projective embedding plus Shapiro/corestriction—proves that this subspace is totally singular. Therefore the true local determinant form is uniformly hyperbolic in every ramified self-dual simple tame module.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The global decorated marked chief-factor induction still needs to be consolidated into a single formal theorem, but the local Kummer/deep-unit ingredient no longer has the filtration-lifting gap.

**NEXT**: Insert the faithful-image projectivity and deep-unit singularity lemmas beside Turn 72’s Wall-doubling proposition, then perform a single dependency audit of the decorated marked induction rather than revisiting individual determinant modules.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
